questions.question: What is the difference between WHERE and HAVING?
options: B) WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY
C) HAVING filters before GROUP BY
questions.correctAnswer: B) WHERE filters rows before grouping; HAVING filters groups after GROUP BY

questions.question: What is the difference between INNER JOIN and LEFT JOIN?
options: B) INNER JOIN returns only matching rows; LEFT JOIN returns all left rows + matching right (NULL if no match)
C) LEFT JOIN returns only left table
D) INNER JOIN includes all rows
questions.correctAnswer: B) INNER JOIN returns only matching rows; LEFT JOIN returns all left rows + matching right (NULL if no match)

questions.question: What is a database index?
options: B) Data structure speeding up data retrieval — trade-off: faster reads, slower writes, more storage
questions.correctAnswer: B) Data structure speeding up data retrieval — trade-off: faster reads, slower writes, more storage

questions.question: What is a PRIMARY KEY?
options: B) Uniquely identifies each row — must be unique and NOT NULL, can be composite
questions.correctAnswer: B) Uniquely identifies each row — must be unique and NOT NULL, can be composite

questions.question: What is database normalization?
options: B) Organizing tables to reduce redundancy and improve integrity — 1NF, 2NF, 3NF forms
questions.correctAnswer: B) Organizing tables to reduce redundancy and improve integrity — 1NF, 2NF, 3NF forms

questions.question: What is a transaction in SQL?
options: B) Sequence of operations treated as single unit — ACID: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability
questions.correctAnswer: B) Sequence of operations treated as single unit — ACID: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability

questions.question: What is the difference between DELETE, TRUNCATE, and DROP?
options: B) DELETE removes rows (logged, reversible); TRUNCATE removes all rows fast (less logged); DROP removes entire table
questions.correctAnswer: B) DELETE removes rows (logged, reversible); TRUNCATE removes all rows fast (less logged); DROP removes entire table

questions.question: What is a subquery?
options: B) Query nested inside another query — can be in SELECT, WHERE, or FROM clause
D) Query on subset of data
questions.correctAnswer: B) Query nested inside another query — can be in SELECT, WHERE, or FROM clause

questions.question: What is a CTE (Common Table Expression)?
options: B) Named temporary result set defined with WITH clause — makes complex queries readable and reusable
questions.correctAnswer: B) Named temporary result set defined with WITH clause — makes complex queries readable and reusable

questions.question: What is the difference between UNION and UNION ALL?
options: B) UNION removes duplicates (slower); UNION ALL keeps all rows including duplicates (faster)
questions.correctAnswer: B) UNION removes duplicates (slower); UNION ALL keeps all rows including duplicates (faster)

questions.question: What is a window function?
options: B) Performs calculation across rows related to current row without collapsing them — ROW_NUMBER, RANK, LAG
questions.correctAnswer: B) Performs calculation across rows related to current row without collapsing them — ROW_NUMBER, RANK, LAG

questions.question: What does EXPLAIN/EXPLAIN ANALYZE do?
options: B) Shows query execution plan — helps identify slow operations, missing indexes, full table scans
questions.correctAnswer: B) Shows query execution plan — helps identify slow operations, missing indexes, full table scans

questions.question: What is a foreign key?
options: B) Column referencing primary key in another table — enforces referential integrity
questions.correctAnswer: B) Column referencing primary key in another table — enforces referential integrity

questions.question: What is SQL injection?
options: B) Attack inserting malicious SQL through user input — prevented by parameterized queries/prepared statements
questions.correctAnswer: B) Attack inserting malicious SQL through user input — prevented by parameterized queries/prepared statements

questions.question: What is the N+1 query problem?
options: B) Fetching parent records (1 query) then separate query for each child — use JOIN or eager loading instead
questions.correctAnswer: B) Fetching parent records (1 query) then separate query for each child — use JOIN or eager loading instead

questions.question: What is database sharding?
options: B) Horizontal partitioning distributing data across multiple database instances based on shard key
questions.correctAnswer: B) Horizontal partitioning distributing data across multiple database instances based on shard key

questions.question: What is the difference between optimistic and pessimistic locking?
options: B) Optimistic assumes no conflict — check on save; Pessimistic locks row immediately on read
questions.correctAnswer: B) Optimistic assumes no conflict — check on save; Pessimistic locks row immediately on read

questions.question: What is a composite index?
options: B) Index on multiple columns — useful for queries filtering on those columns together; column order matters
questions.correctAnswer: B) Index on multiple columns — useful for queries filtering on those columns together; column order matters

questions.question: What does COALESCE() do?
options: B) Returns first non-NULL value from its arguments — used for NULL handling/default values
questions.correctAnswer: B) Returns first non-NULL value from its arguments — used for NULL handling/default values

questions.question: What is database denormalization?
options: B) Intentionally adding redundancy to improve read performance — trade-off against update anomalies
questions.correctAnswer: B) Intentionally adding redundancy to improve read performance — trade-off against update anomalies

questions.question: Which is not a NoSQL database?
questions.correctAnswer: C) MySQL

questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter groups?
questions.correctAnswer: B) HAVING

questions.question: Which SQL command deletes table?
questions.correctAnswer: B) DROP
